reviewer.evidencePolicy: Apply refreshed evidence only to the seven remaining stale claims covered by current focused tests, live command packets, checked-in contract citations, and bounded non-claim statements.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names a dev/skill eval path over a fixture that contains multi-turn `turns`.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by running `cautilus eval test --runtime fixture` through the self-dogfood refresh-flow adapter after fixing the adapter template to pass the checked-in fixture results file. The resulting eval-cases packet preserves two turns and the eval-observed/eval-summary packets complete the fixture-backed eval path.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep this scoped to fixture-backed multi-turn eval; use Codex/Claude dogfood evidence for semantic runtime quality claims.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names machine-readable packet families and agent-consumable fields.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused packet-flow tests covering eval, report, review, evidence, optimize, proposal, and revision packet schema families.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep HTML projection proof separate from this machine-readable packet family claim.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names a deterministic packet projection: full proposal list plus bounded attentionView.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused scenario propose tests and live proposal output showing a full proposals packet with bounded attentionView keys derived from the ranked proposal set.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep attentionView bounded without hiding the full ranked proposals list from agents.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic scenario conversation review packet linkage over normalized chatbot threads.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused scenario review-conversations tests and live output showing linked normalized thread records, linked proposals, existing coverage hints, and bounded attention reasons.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Do not treat this as proof that live operator turns were crawled; the command consumes normalized input packets.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic JSON span extraction into a rendered review prompt.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused review prompt tests and CLI review variants smoke covering `--output-text-key` extraction into `## Output Under Test Text`.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep judge-quality claims separate from output-under-test text extraction.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names deterministic local gates, checked-in GitHub workflow wiring, and external consumer onboarding smoke.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by checked-in verify workflow/script wiring and a live `npm run consumer:onboard:smoke` that completed install, adapter init, minimal wiring, adapter resolve, doctor ready, and one bounded eval test with accept-now recommendation.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep this as onboarding smoke proof, not broad external consumer product-behavior proof.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim combines deterministic scenario-normalizer completeness gates with a human-auditable surface/preset vocabulary contract.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by `lint:specs`, `lint:scenario-normalizers`, direct scenario normalizer completeness output, and maintainer spec citations that keep scenario normalization aligned with the current dev/app surface and repo/skill/chat/prompt preset vocabulary.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Continue treating new broad user-facing copy as requiring spec alignment review, not just scenario-normalizer lint.
